Notice.Title: Barbican Fire Project - High Sensitive Fire Doors
Notice.Description:
The City of London are seeking a Competent Contractor to undertake the fire remediation works to the Heritage Doors only. The 2019 Surveys are deemed out of date, and as such, the successful Contractor will be responsible for the remediation (repair or replacement) strategy for all 354 doors.
The scope of works is broadly defined as:
- Remediation to existing fire doors/frames as appropriate - Replacement of existing fire doors/frames as appropriate - All other reasonably foreseeable works required to complete the above

AwardDetail.SupplierAddress: Suite 4, lst Floor, Howbury House, Thames Road, Dartford, Kent, DA1 4RQ, UNITED KINGDOM OF GREAT BRITAIN AND NORTHERN IRELAND

Notice.Title: Fire Remediation Works with Aster Group

Works are to address actions arising from Specialist Fire Safety Reports. Direct Award via LHC Building and Fire Safety Framework Lot WS4A - Installation, Maintenance & Remediation of Passive Fire Protection
Notice.CpvDescription: Fire-prevention installation works and Installation of doors

Notice.Title: 26025 Soil Affected Roads Innovation Trial (Further Competition under EHF 4, Lot 1)
Notice.Description: The outline scope of the services required is a Soil Affected Roads Innovation Trial, a carriageway reconstruction scheme utilising multiple unique treatments to the B1099 Upwell Road March. Treatments will range from geopolymer resin injection, deep soil mixing, steel carriageway reinforcement and geogrid reconstruction. We will be looking for some added value in the form of sustainability (carbon reduction and reporting) to meet CCC's Climate Change and Environment Strategy.

AwardDetail.SupplierAddress: Brampton House, 19 Tenter Road, Moulton Park, Northampton, Northamptonshire, NN3 6PZ

Notice.Title: Voids & Repairs Works for St Leger Homes of Doncaster on behalf of the City of Doncaster Council
Notice.Description:
Delivery of Voids & Repairs works that cannot be completed internally by St Leger Homes of Doncaster. The service supports internal capacity and ensures timely, quality completion of works.
The contract is for an initial period of 2 years, with the available option to extend for a further 2 x 12 months at the sole discretion of St Leger Homes & Doncaster Council.
This underlying contract is awarded via Mini Competition from (Lot 9 - General Building Works (Including but not limited to remodelling, refurbishment and small extensions)) of the EN:Procure Dynamic Purchasing System Agreement for the provision of Major and Minor Construction Works. (OJEU Contract Notice 2025/S 000-005834).

AwardDetail.SupplierAddress: Palmer Street, Hyde Park, Doncaster, South Yorkshire, DN4 5DD

Notice.Title: Lisbon HMA New Residence 2
Notice.Description: The Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Office (FCDO) sought a main works construction contractor to undertake the refurbishment and fit-out of the new Residence for His Majesty's Ambassador in Lisbon, Portugal. The property will provide residential accommodation for the Ambassador and support diplomatic functions, including formal events and occasional accommodation for visiting VIPs. The works comprised significant refurbishment, alteration and fit-out works required to make the property suitable for occupation and official diplomatic use. Appropriate suppliers appointed to the FCDO DPS within the relevant value band were invited to submit tenders for the provision of the above works. The procurement was conducted as a further competition under the DPS in accordance with PCR 2015. Tenders were evaluated on the basis of Most Economically Advantageous Tender (MEAT), considering both quality and price in accordance with the published evaluation criteria. Orostream International Contracts Limited were appointed as the Successful Supplier, having submitted the Most Economically Advantageous Tender.
Notice.CpvDescription: Building construction work and Refurbishment work

AwardDetail.SupplierAddress: Beacon House, Croft Road, Crowborough, East Sussex, TN6 1DL

CpvCode.Description: Construction structures and materials; auxiliary products to construction (except electric apparatus)

Notice.Title: Temporary Scaffolding Handrails to Failing Concrete Balustrades and Walkways - Incommunities Limited
Notice.Description:
The Employer, following a structural engineer's report identifying defects to existing concrete balustrades and walkways, requires the provision of a suitably experiences and qualified Scaffolding Contractor to provide designs, supply, erect, maintain and dismantle temporary scaffolding systems to provide safe, continuous temporary handrails to all affected areas within occupied residential blocks.
The temporary works are required to mitigate fall risks and ensure resident safety pending permanent remedial works.
The works shall include, but not be limited to:
- Site inspections and survey of all affected walkways and balustrades. - Liaison with the Employer / Managing agent regarding access and sequencing. - Design of temporary scaffolding handrail systems. - Supply and installation of all scaffold components. - Provision of edge protection to exposed edges. - Routine inspection, maintenance and adjustment. - Safe dismantling and making good on completion.
The Contract is let on a JCT Measured Term Contract 2016 basis with a contract term of 12 months commencing on the 6th July 2026 with an option to extend the contract of up to a further 2nr 12 months up to the 5th July 2029 at the absolute sole discrestion of the Employer.
There is no volume or workflow guarantee, but indicatively it is anticipated that orders for works between £0 and £200,000 per annum may be issued via this contract.
The initial works to be undertaken to the following blocks in Year 1:
Kelvin House, Danecourt Road, Bradford, BD4 0DE: A 9-storey and plant room GF-8F purpose-built, single stairway, detached flats tower block containing 57 flats. The building consists of 2 blocks arranged in an offset H shape, with the two (2) blocks joined together at upper floor levels by semi-enclosed bridges.
Ogden House, Danecourt Road, Bradford, BD4 0DF: A 9 storey (B-8F) purpose-built single stairway detached flats tower block containing 55 flats and offices. The building consists of two separate blocks arranged in an H shape, with the two blocks joined together at upper floor levels by semi-enclosed bridges. The bridges contain a lift, protected stairway enclosure.
Further works and future Years: Incommunities reserves the right to include similar type works to other HRBs within its portfolio. At Incommunities request, the same detail, design and site surveys are to be carried out for the following HRBs;
Thorpe Edge Blocks: Gerard House, Fairhaven Green, Bradford, BD10 9QU Ailsa House, 65 Fairhaven Green, Bradford, BD10 9ND Thorold House, Fairhaven Green, Bradford, BD10 9QT Waryn House, Haigh Beck View, Bradford, BD10 9NE
This underlying contract is awarded via Mini Competition from Lot 1 - Scaffolding and Associated Services of the EN:Procure Dynamic Purchasing System (DPS) for Scaffolding and Associated Services (OJEU Contract Notice 2025/S 000-005663).

AwardDetail.SupplierAddress: 2 MONUMENT PARK, PATTINSON INDUSTRIAL ESTATE, WASHINGTON, TYNE & WEAR, NE38 8QU

NN5 7UG

Notice.Title: Old Westgate Station Hotel Development, Wakefield
Notice.Description:
Wakefield Council undertook a procurement process, pursuant to Regulation 30 of the Public Contracts Regulations 2015 to procure and appoint a development partner to deliver its hotel regeneration ambitions for the Old Westgate Station site in Wakefield City Centre.
Ion Property Developments Limited was the most economically advantageous tenderer and was subsequent appointed as Wakefield Council's preferred partner.
Wakefield Council entered into a pre-development services agreement ("PDSA") on 25 February 2026 and, Wakefield Council intends to enter into a development agreement ("DA"), in relation to the delivery of the Old Westgate Station Hotel Development, in Wakefield City Centre.
Notice.CpvDescription: Site preparation work and Hotel construction work and Building completion work

AwardDetail.SupplierAddress: Port of Liverpool Building, First Floor, Liverpool, United Kingdom, L3 1BY
AwardDetail.AdditionalDetail:
A F20 Modification Notice cannot be published at this time. However, to ensure transparency and compliance with legislative requirements, the details of the modification are set out below.
By transfer deed dated 31 March 2023 Network Rail Infrastructure Limited transferred a piece of land to Wakefield Council which includes the structure of bridge deck, which is a single structure which runs under and supports the East Coast Mainline ("Bridge Deck"). The transfer provides that Wakefield Council (and any successor in title) will be obligated to repair Bridge Deck.
The Information Memorandum ("IM"), released alongside the Selection Questionnaire ("SQ"), set out Wakefield Council's position on Bridge Deck which confirmed that Bridge Deck would transfer to the successful bidder, as part of the Old Westgate Station Hotel development, with this position to be set out in the contractual documents.
Owing to unforeseen circumstances, in respect of the provision of funding for the development the Old Westgate Station Hotel development, Wakefield Council intends to modify the DA so that Bridge Deck will not transfer to Ion Property Developments Limited.
The modification (i.e. that Bridge Deck will not transfer to the successful bidder) has been brought about by circumstances, in respect of third party funding constraints, which Wakefield Council, acting as a diligent contracting authority could not have foreseen. The modification does not alter the overall nature of the contractual documents and there is no increase in price as a result of the intended modification.
Need for modification brought about by circumstances that a diligent contracting/entity could not foresee.
Updated total contract value before the modifications (taking into account possible earlier contract modifications, price adaptions and average inflation) - Estimated total value: £8,000,000 (excluding VAT)
Total contract value after the modifications - Estimated total value: £8,000,000 (excluding VAT)
